repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: vim.NIL is truthy in Lua, so `#(result.items or result)`
crashes on `#vim.NIL` when servers return null.

Solution: skip spec-allowed result=null silently, raise an error
on items=null with the server name.

https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/#textDocument_completion

Fix #39400

comment_data.body: if https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39400 is a server bug then this should be `assert` or `error`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1004,7 +1004,14 @@ local function trigger(bufnr, clients, ctx)
       end
 
       local result = response.result
-      if result and #(result.items or result) > 0 then
+      if type(result) == 'table' and result.items == vim.NIL then
+        vim.notify_once(
+          ('%s: completion response has items=null, expected CompletionItem[]'):format(

comment_data.body: This would interrupt the user's experience.  response error also thrown via notify.

comment_data.body: checking `result ~= vim.NIL` here is another change? is `result == vim.NIL` a spec violation or not? the PR description does not mention this.

+        vim.notify_once(

+            client and client.name or 'UNKNOWN'

+          vim.log.levels.ERROR
+        )

comment_data.body:
result can be null so added a check here . updated commit msg

```
result: CompletionItem[] | [CompletionList] | null. 
If a CompletionItem[] is provided it is interpreted to be complete. 
So it is the same as { isIncomplete: false, items }
```

comment_data.body:
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39400 also interrupts the user's experience. The point of the discussion there was to show a more meaningful error message, since you said we shouldn't handle it. If we're not going to handle/support/allow it, then we use assert/error to show a more meaningful error. 

`vim.notify*` is a "soft" message and must not be used for errors.

Some existing lsp code uses `vim.notify` where it shouldn't. That's a separate topic...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1004,7 +1004,14 @@ local function trigger(bufnr, clients, ctx)
       end
 
       local result = response.result
-      if result and #(result.items or result) > 0 then
+      if type(result) == 'table' and result.items == vim.NIL then
+        vim.notify_once(
+          ('%s: completion response has items=null, expected CompletionItem[]'):format(

comment_data.body:
It’s a server-side issue, but using `assert/error` interrupts the user’s workflow. They can’t continue coding just because the server returned something invalid. Why should we break the user experience for a server error?

Wouldn’t it be better to surface a notification and simply skip the invalid response? In completion.lua, errors are  handled via notify, which avoids disrupting the user. 😮‍💨   If you strongly prefer using assert/error here, I don’t have any objections.
